description: This figure presents comprehensive (S)TEM characterization of the initial microstructure in an Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel. Subfigures (a1) through (a8) show STEM-EDS elemental maps for Fe, Mn, Al, C, V, and Ni, plus a composite overlay of Al, V, C, and Ni, revealing compositional partitioning between the austenite and B2 phases. (b) displays a high-resolution TEM image of the semi-coherent interface between the B2 and austenite (γ) phases, with the inset fast Fourier transform pattern indicating an orientation relationship of [011]γ // [111]B2. (c) provides an HRTEM image of an individual coherent VC precipitate within the austenite matrix, with the inset selected-area electron diffraction pattern confirming [011]γ // [011]VC. (d) is a dark-field TEM image taken from the L′12 superlattice reflection, verifying the presence of L′12-type long-range ordered nanodomains. (e) presents an inverse FFT image with the L′12 ordered nanodomains highlighted by red circles. Collectively, these results establish a duplex austenite-B2 microstructure featuring dual-nanoprecipitation of coherent VC carbides and ultrafine L′12 LRO domains, providing critical nanostructural evidence for the multiple strengthening mechanisms.
